cards.hook: Objective reality might not exist. A real experiment says so.
cards.caption: Objective reality might not exist. A quantum test let two observers record different "true" outcomes of the same event. Physics-breaking stuff my feed pulls before the timeline finds it.
cards.stance: If two observers can both be right about contradictory facts, "objective" was always a comforting story.

cards.hook: "Negative time" is real. Photons exited before they entered.
cards.caption: "Negative time" is real: photons left a material before they finished entering it. Not a glitch, a measured result. The wait-what science my feed keeps and the noise it drops.

cards.hook: Time isn't fundamental. It emerges from entanglement.
cards.caption: Time isn't a given, it emerges from quantum entanglement between systems. The universe may not have a clock underneath. Deep-cut physics my feed surfaces before the timeline.
cards.stance: We treat time as the stage. The physics says it's just a side effect.

cards.hook: Cloudflare + Chrome ran real post-quantum TLS. At scale.
cards.caption: Post-quantum encryption isn't theory anymore. Cloudflare and Chrome ran quantum-resistant TLS on live traffic. Your future security is already shipping. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: "Quantum will break encryption someday" is outdated. The fix is already in production.

cards.hook: Cloudflare + Chrome ran real post-quantum TLS. At scale.
cards.caption: Duplicate of card-04 (same Cloudflare post-quantum TLS post). Dropping to avoid posting the same link twice.

cards.hook: Time might wobble. And that finally makes gravity testable.
cards.caption: Post-quantum gravity says time itself wobbles, and unlike string theory, you can actually test it. A rival theory of everything you can falsify. The science my feed keeps.
cards.stance: String theory promised everything and tested nothing. This one bets on being provable.

cards.hook: Molecules big enough to see just existed in two places at once.
cards.caption: The biggest molecules ever were just put in two places at once. Quantum weirdness is climbing toward our scale. Where does it stop? The wait-what findings my feed pulls first.

cards.hook: AI is designing quantum experiments no human ever imagined.
cards.caption: An AI built to crunch numbers is now inventing quantum experiments physicists couldn't conceive. The tool started leading the science. Found before the timeline, kept by my feed.
cards.stance: We built AI to speed up our ideas. It's quietly having its own.

cards.hook: Reality doesn't exist until you measure it.
cards.caption: Reality doesn't exist until you measure it. Australian physicists re-ran a famous quantum experiment and confirmed it. The stuff my feed keeps while the timeline scrolls past.
cards.stance: If observation creates reality, "objective" physics is doing a lot of heavy lifting.

cards.hook: Reality doesn't exist until it's measured, says the ANU.
cards.caption: Reality doesn't exist until it's measured, an ANU quantum experiment finds. 227 likes, 239 replies arguing about it. Found before the timeline caught on.

cards.hook: Some problems need an "exotic" quantum proof. No document will do.
cards.caption: Some math problems can't be checked with any written proof. You need a quantum object. Researchers just confirmed it. The wild stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: A spooky quantum experiment just proved Einstein wrong.
cards.caption: Measuring a photon changes where it is. The 'spooky' experiment Einstein hated keeps winning. Found before the timeline caught on.
cards.stance: Einstein was right about almost everything and wrong about the one thing that mattered most.

cards.hook: The experiment that proved 'spooky action' is real.
cards.caption: Quantum theory said particles could affect each other instantly. This experiment proved it. 47 likes, 44 replies still arguing. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: Spectroscopy sounds nerdy. It's the study of everything.
cards.caption: Spectroscopy sounds nerdy, but it's how we learned the universe runs on quantum rules before anyone told us. Quietly the study of everything. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: Most 'quantum computing' headlines are describing machines that don't exist.
cards.caption: The quantum computers in the headlines are almost always theoretical. The ones that exist are tiny. A researcher's reality check my feed kept.
cards.stance: Half of 'quantum breakthrough' coverage is describing hardware nobody has built yet.

cards.hook: Gravity probably isn't what collapses the quantum world.
cards.caption: A leading theory said gravity forces quantum states to 'pick' reality. New experiment says: unlikely. 127 likes deep in the debate. Found before the timeline.

cards.hook: Physicists just measured light spending "negative time"
cards.caption: Physicists measured light spending "negative time" inside a material. It exits before it should have entered. Your intuition is wrong and the math is right. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: "Negative time" isn't a glitch or a headline trick -- it's a real measured delay, and it breaks how you picture cause and effect.

cards.hook: Einstein called it "spooky." This experiment proved it real.
cards.caption: Einstein hated quantum entanglement -- he called it "spooky action at a distance." A Griffith University experiment just verified it. He was right about the spookiness, wrong to doubt it. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The smartest physicist who ever lived bet against entanglement and lost.

cards.hook: Heat just flowed cold-to-hot. The arrow of time reversed.
cards.caption: In a quantum system, heat flowed backward -- cold to hot -- reversing the arrow of time. Everything you were taught about entropy assumes this can't happen. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The second law of thermodynamics feels absolute until a quantum system runs it in reverse.

cards.hook: Physicists measured light spending "negative time" in a material
cards.caption: Light was measured spending "negative time" inside a material -- exiting before it entered. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: A light particle left a cloud before it entered it.
cards.caption: A light particle exited a cloud before it entered -- scientists measured a negative amount of time inside. Quantum physics melts your brain and it's supposed to. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The weirdest results in physics aren't errors -- they're the universe telling you your intuition was never built for this.

cards.hook: Quantum proofs beat classical ones. It's now proven.
cards.caption: After 20 years, researchers have strong evidence quantum proofs are strictly more powerful than classical ones -- no way around the quantum world's complexity. This is a big deal for computing. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: There are truths a classical computer can verify but never efficiently -- and quantum machines just walked past that wall.

cards.hook: A quantum experiment just simulated a working time machine.
cards.caption: A quantum experiment just simulated a working time machine. Not sci-fi — a real lab result on closed timelike curves. The kind of paper my feed keeps and the timeline buries.
cards.stance: Time travel isn't banned by physics — it's banned by our budget for qubits.

cards.hook: Ask physicists if quantum mechanics needs imaginary numbers — they split 50/50.
cards.caption: Ask physicists if quantum mechanics needs imaginary numbers, and they split 50/50. One of the deepest questions in the field has no consensus. Found before the timeline noticed.
cards.stance: If the experts can't agree whether i is real, your intuition about quantum was never the problem.

cards.hook: CERN found a superposition that occupies two states at once.
cards.caption: CERN reports a new form of quantum superposition — one thing in two states at once, for real. Your intuition breaks, the math doesn't. The stuff my feed keeps over the memes.

cards.hook: Physicists just measured negative time in a quantum experiment.
cards.caption: Physicists measured 'negative time' — photons that exit a cloud before they finish entering it. Not a typo, a real result. The wait-what finding my feed pulled first.
cards.stance: Negative time sounds fake until you realize causality was always more negotiable than you were taught.

cards.hook: You can build a real quantum entanglement experiment at home.
cards.caption: You can build a real quantum entanglement rig at home — Scientific American wrote the guide. Spooky action, on your desk. The kind of build my feed keeps for you.

cards.hook: Time might not be fundamental — just entanglement in disguise.
cards.caption: Scientists reversed local entropy with quantum computers, hinting time is an emergent property of entanglement, not a law. Physics is more flexible than you were told. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Time's arrow isn't a rule of the universe — it's a side effect of how things get entangled.

cards.hook: Simulating a simulation: algorithms for quantum computing experiments.
cards.caption: A paper on algorithms for simulating quantum computing experiments. Deep for practitioners, but a bare PDF link with no payoff line — too niche and dry to travel.

cards.hook: Quantum imaging could push exoplanet photos to the physical limit.
cards.caption: Quantum information theory could sharpen exoplanet imaging all the way to the theoretical limit — sharper alien-world photos from pure math. The space-tech find my feed keeps.
